steps.title: Identify the counterintuitive signal
steps.content: The passage explicitly labels this a “counterintuitive effect” — the key signal that demand moved opposite to what normal economics predicts. Normally, higher prices kill demand; the next sentence reveals that demand actually increased (buyers “thronged the market”). The blank must describe what happened to demand: it was created or produced, not destroyed.

steps.title: Match the meaning to the effect
steps.content: Engendering means to produce or cause, so “engendering demand” describes how rising prices actually created demand — the exact counterintuitive reversal. Monetizing refers to converting to profit, appraising refers to assessing value, and exploiting refers to taking unfair advantage — none describe what happened to the level of demand itself. That matches choice A — A is correct.
